page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 8 dedicated physical cores built on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with single-threaded execution per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8gn.2xlarge demonstrates strong memory subsystem performance, with its Passmark memory score placing in the top tier (top 10%). Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 925.05 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 153.96 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write speed of 86.14 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing both rank in the top 25%. However, the ARMv9 architecture exhibits some performance trade-offs; the Passmark CPU encryption test falls into the bottom 25%, and LLM inference tasks show mixed results, with medium model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gn.2xlarge leverages the high resource density of the Graviton ARM architecture to deliver competitive memory-per-core value without the overhead of hyperthreading. The absence of local NVMe storage means storage costs are decoupled, which is suitable for workloads utilizing external EBS volumes. This instance is designed for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, text processing pipelines, and general-purpose memory-optimized workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, utilizing a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external connectivity. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 10% and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds. Conversely, encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing represent performance trade-offs, scoring in the lower percentiles. This profile makes the r8gn.2xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including Redis caching, database operations, and text processing, where high memory bandwidth and dedicated ARM cores offer qualitative resource efficiency.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized instance delivering high-bandwidth memory performance and dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz.
64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier memory performance with Passmark memory score in the top 10%.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage.
Suited for Redis caching, database operations, and text processing.
